page: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with a 40 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache. The hardware configuration includes 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding 56.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 459 GB of local storage. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate that the GS4-4 exhibits weak to poor processing performance. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, fall into the bottom 10% of tested servers. Similarly, Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores rank in the bottom 25%. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, reaching 336.87 GB/sec, though large block read and write bandwidths drop to weak and poor categories respectively. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the bottom 25%, reflecting the older CPU architecture's throughput limitations.

Given its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio of 56 GB per core, the GS4-4 is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high compute density. The qualitative cost profile reflects a high resource density in terms of memory capacity relative to its modest processing power. This makes the server suitable for large in-memory caching, legacy database hosting, or memory-bound data processing tasks where CPU execution speed is not the primary bottleneck.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 2.0 GHz Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 224.0 GB (56.0 GB per core) and 459 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks show weak to poor CPU throughput, with stress-ng scores in the bottom 10% and Geekbench scores in the bottom 25%. However, it maintains average memory bandwidth for small cached blocks at 336.87 GB/sec. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads, making it suitable for in-memory caching, legacy databases, and memory-bound data processing where massive RAM is required but high CPU performance is not critical.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering massive memory density and local storage for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute requirements.
